title: xanthium orientale subsp. italicum
intro: Xanthium orientale subsp. italicum, also known as cocklebur, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, characterized by its distinctive burs and prickly stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as xanthium saponins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Xanthium orientale subsp. italicum have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and pain relief, as well as antimicrobial and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is generally recommended in moderation due to potential side effects.
